{
  "playbook": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "phase": "IMMEDIATE",
      "responsible_team": "Security Operations Center (SOC) / DevOps",
      "action": "Confirm the incident alert and establish a dedicated communication channel (e.g., bridge call, secure chat). Notify all relevant stakeholders (e.g., CISO, IT Management, Legal, PR). This step is foundational for coordinated response.",
      "success_criteria": "Incident declared, communication bridge active, key stakeholders informed.",
      "estimated_time": "15 minutes",
      "rollback_procedure": "N/A (Communication step)"
    },
    {
      "step": 2,
      "phase": "IMMEDIATE",
      "responsible_team": "Network Operations / Platform Team / DevOps",
      "action": "Isolate the target server (logical 'State Bank of India' core services) to a quarantined network segment. Restrict all non-essential inbound and outbound traffic. Allow only specific, secured access for incident response team's jump boxes/tools. This prevents further compromise and limits blast radius.",
      "commands": [
        "### For Cloud Environments (e.g., AWS Security Groups):",
        "# 1. Identify the instance ID(s) associated with the 'State Bank of India' core services.",
        "INSTANCE_IDS=\"i-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx i-yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy\"",
        "echo \"Identified instances: $INSTANCE_IDS\"",
        "# 2. Identify existing Security Groups (SGs) for these instances.",
        "for ID in $INSTANCE_IDS; do aws ec2 describe-instances --instance-ids $ID --query \"Reservations[].Instances[].SecurityGroups[].GroupId\" --output text; done",
        "# 3. Create a new, highly restrictive quarantine Security Group.",
        "QUARANTINE_SG_ID=$(aws ec2 create-security-group --group-name \"sbi-core-quarantine-sg\" --description \"Quarantine SG for SBI Core Server Incident\" --vpc-id vpc-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx --query \"GroupId\" --output text)",
        "echo \"Created quarantine SG: $QUARANTINE_SG_ID\"",
        "# 4. Add minimal inbound rules for Incident Response (IR) team's jump box IP (replace with actual IP).",
        "IR_JUMP_BOX_IP=\"10.0.0.100/32\"",
        "aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $QUARANTINE_SG_ID --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr $IR_JUMP_BOX_IP --description \"Allow SSH for IR Team\"",
        "aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress --group-id $QUARANTINE_SG_ID --protocol tcp --port 443 --cidr $IR_JUMP_BOX_IP --description \"Allow HTTPS for IR Tools\"",
        "# 5. Modify instance(s) to use ONLY the new quarantine SG. This will remove all previous SGs.",
        "for ID in $INSTANCE_IDS; do aws ec2 modify-instance-attribute --instance-id $ID --groups $QUARANTINE_SG_ID; done",
        "",
        "### For On-Premise Linux Servers (e.g., using iptables):",
        "# 1. Backup current iptables rules.",
        "sudo iptables-save > /etc/sysconfig/iptables.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)",
        "# 2. Set default policies to DROP.",
        "sudo iptables -P INPUT DROP",
        "sudo iptables -P FORWARD DROP",
        "sudo iptables -P OUTPUT DROP",
        "# 3. Allow loopback traffic.",
        "sudo iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT",
        "sudo iptables -A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT",
        "# 4. Allow SSH from IR jump box IP (replace with actual IP).",
        "IR_JUMP_BOX_IP=\"10.0.0.100\"",
        "sudo iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -s $IR_JUMP_BOX_IP -j ACCEPT",
        "# 5. Allow established connections.",
        "sudo iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT",
        "sudo iptables -A OUTPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT",
        "# 6. Save rules (for RHEL/CentOS, adjust for Debian/Ubuntu if using persistent iptables).",
        "sudo service iptables save"
      ],
      "success_criteria": "Network connectivity to the server is restricted to authorized incident response channels only. `ping` from unauthorized IPs fails. `ssh` from IR jump box succeeds. Application services are inaccessible from external networks.",
      "estimated_time": "30 minutes",
      "rollback_procedure": "### For Cloud Environments:",
      "rollback_commands": [
        "# 1. Identify the original Security Group IDs (from Step 2, point 2 or configuration management).",
        "ORIGINAL_SG_IDS=\"sg-original-1 sg-original-2\"",
        "# 2. Revert instance(s) to original Security Groups.",
        "for ID in $INSTANCE_IDS; do aws ec2 modify-instance-attribute --instance-id $ID --groups $ORIGINAL_SG_IDS; done",
        "# 3. (Optional) Delete the quarantine SG if no longer needed.",
        "aws ec2 delete-security-group --group-id $QUARANTINE_SG_ID"
      ],
      "rollback_procedure_on_prem": [
        "### For On-Premise Linux Servers:",
        "# 1. Restore iptables from backup.",
        "sudo iptables-restore < /etc/sysconfig/iptables.bak.<timestamp>",
        "# 2. Restart iptables service.",
        "sudo service iptables restart"
      ]
    },

      "action": "Create a full disk snapshot or image of the critical server(s) for forensic analysis. This preserves the current state of the system for investigation without altering the live environment.",
      "commands": [

        "echo \"Instance ID: $INSTANCE_ID, Root Volume ID: $VOLUME_ID\"",
        "# 2. Create a snapshot of the root volume.",
        "SNAPSHOT_ID=$(aws ec2 create-snapshot --volume-id $VOLUME_ID --description \"Forensic snapshot for SBI Core Server incident - $(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)\" --query \"SnapshotId\" --output text)",
        "echo \"Snapshot created: $SNAPSHOT_ID\"",
        "# 3. (Optional but recommended) Create an AMI from the instance for easier restoration/cloning.",
        "AMI_ID=$(aws ec2 create-image --instance-id $INSTANCE_ID --name \"Forensic-AMI-SBI-Core-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)\" --description \"AMI for forensic analysis of SBI Core Server\" --no-reboot --query \"ImageId\" --output text)",
        "echo \"AMI created: $AMI_ID\"",
        "",
        "### For On-Premise Virtual Machines (e.g., VMware vSphere, Hyper-V):",
        "# Use the hypervisor management console (vCenter, Hyper-V Manager) to create a snapshot or clone of the VM.",
        "# Ensure the snapshot is 'quiesced' if possible to ensure data consistency."
      ],
      "success_criteria": "Snapshot/AMI creation job initiated and completed successfully. Snapshot ID/AMI ID recorded and verified in the cloud console or hypervisor manager.",
      "estimated_time": "30 minutes (creation time varies with disk size)",

        "<!-- Add this appender -->",

        "</appender>",
